name: HUBBELL INC
business: Hubbell is a global manufacturer of electrical products and utility solutions, serving customers through its Utility Solutions segment (63% of revenues) and Electrical Solutions segment (37% of revenues). The Utility Solutions segment provides components for grid transmission, distribution, and smart grid applications, while the Electrical Solutions segment offers wiring devices, connectors, industrial controls, and other electrical equipment for commercial, industrial, and residential markets.

exposures.sentence: Electrical Solutions segment products have applications in the non-residential, light industrial, heavy industrial, datacenter, electric transmission and distribution, and renewables markets.
Electrical Solutions segment products have applications in the non-residential, light industrial, heavy industrial, datacenter, electric transmission and distribution, and renewables markets.
This includes utility transmission & distribution (T&D) components such as arresters, insulators, connectors, anchors, bushings, enclosures, cutouts and switches.

Hubbell Utility Solutions supports the electrical distribution, electrical substation, electrical transmission, gas distribution, telecommunications, utility meters & AMI, and grid protection and controls markets.
Harsh and hazardous products are primarily used in the oil and gas (onshore and offshore) and mining industries.
Harsh and hazardous products are primarily used in the oil and gas (onshore and offshore) and mining industries.
certain of our businesses design and manufacture industrial controls and communication systems used in the non-residential and industrial markets.
The Utility Solutions segment also offers solutions that serve The Edge of the utility infrastructure, including smart meters, communications systems, and protection and control devices.
We provide utility and electrical solutions that enable our customers to operate critical infrastructure reliably and efficiently.

Our information technology systems are susceptible to cyber threats, malware, phishing attacks, break-ins and similar events, breaches of physical security or tampering and manipulation of these systems by employees or unauthorized third parties.
Advancements in, and increased adoption of, artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning, automation, and other advanced technologies may result in a long-term competitive disadvantage.
